hypothesis: Incorporating episodic memory of past simulation failures and parameter extraction errors into the multi-agent framework—such that agents can retrieve and condition their behavior on structurally similar prior cases—will measurably reduce the rate of task-specific configuration errors (e.g., incorrect mixture versus individual adsorbate setups, misassigned force field parameters from non-standard table layouts) compared to the current prompt-only architecture, as quantified by a reduction in failure rate across repeated runs on held-out simulation tasks.
